label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to analyze this ethical dilemma where a pharmaceutical employee discovers a harmful side effect of a new drug, but the company wants to hide it to protect revenue. The employee has to decide whether to follow company rules or speak out. Let me start by breaking down the elements here. First, the main parties involved are the employee, the company, the public, and potentially patients who will take the drug. The company has strict rules against sharing confidential info, which is probably to protect their proprietary information and avoid legal issues. But the employee finds out about a harmful side effect and the company decides to keep it secret to maintain profits. So the dilemma is between loyalty to the company (and following their rules) versus the ethical obligation to protect public health. I need to consider different ethical frameworks here. Maybe utilitarianism, deontological ethics, virtue ethics, and also legal and professional standards. Utilitarianism
